mission: Reduce any future non-neutral charged surface extension to its exact minimal datum by using global holonomy closure on the punctured sphere.

theorem_1_compact_sphere_holonomy_closur.statement: For a punctured U(1) connection on the compact horizon sphere, the loop winding once around all punctures is contractible in S^2 - V. Therefore the product of puncture holonomies must be trivial, equivalently the total puncture charge vanishes mod k.

formulas.puncture_holonomies: h_i = exp(2 pi i a_i / k)

theorem_2_minimal_nonneutral_extension_r.statement: Any future extension that wants a visible nonzero total puncture charge q_vis on a compact horizon sphere must introduce an additional compensating sector carrying charge q_comp = -q_vis mod k. Minimality forces this extra sector to contribute only that single diagonal charge datum. Equivalently, the minimal non-neutral extension is classified by one compensator charge q in Z_k \ {0}.

formulas.visible_charge: q_vis = sum_{i in vis} a_i mod k

theorem_3_current_stack_has_no_compensat.statement: The present stack derives neither an extra marked puncture with distinguished physical role, nor an open boundary/edge mode, nor any separate compensator carrier. Therefore the current-stack value of the compensator charge is zero, and no non-neutral visible surface sector is present.

imports.charged_boundary: After canonical spin normalization, every remaining charged current-stack route is exhausted. There is no physical non-neutral surface sector, no derived transfer from puncture-moduli line data to an extra determinant line on S^2, and no hidden CP-odd inflow inside the standard surface Chern-Simons sector. The only positive future route is genuinely new charged boundary physics beyond the present stack.

final_boundary.safe_statement: Any positive non-neutral Paper 35 surface extension is now reduced to one exact datum: a compensator charge q in Z_k \ {0}. On the current compact closed-sphere stack, that sector is absent. So future work should target an explicit compensator defect / edge-mode theorem rather than a vague non-neutral surface sector.
